title: pedicularis szetschuanica var. typica
intro: Pedicularis szetschuanica var. typica, also known as Szechuan Pedunculate Lousewort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which may provide health benefits including relieving coughs and reducing swelling.
